mnemonics.title: Fluorosis Types
mnemonics.content: DENTAL affects Teeth, SKELETAL affects Bones. D for Decalcification in Dental, S for Stiffness in Skeletal.

mnemonics.title: Safe Fluoride Range
mnemonics.content: Think of 15 - (1.5 mg/L is the limit, remember '15' from BIS standard).

mnemonics.title: Nalgonda Technique
mnemonics.content: ALUM + LIME = FLUORIDE REMOVAL (A for Adsorb, L for Lime precipitates).

mnemonics.title: Fluorosis Symptoms
mnemonics.content: SPOTS on teeth, STIFF joints, PAIN in bones - S-S-P mnemonic.

mnemonics.title: Fluoride Sources
mnemonics.content: Fluoride comes from ROCKS - Rocks Originate Potassium (Fluorite), Calcium compounds.

acronyms.usage: Sets drinking water quality standards including fluoride limits (1.5 mg/L)

acronyms.expansion: National Environmental Engineering Research Institute (NEERI) developed method
acronyms.usage: Low-cost defluoridation using alum and lime for rural areas
Membrane technology that removes 90-95% of fluoride from water
Sets international guidelines for safe fluoride levels in drinking water
Activated carbon used in some filtration systems for water purification

visualTips.visualization: Think of a thermometer: below 0.5 (too cold = caries), 0.5-1.5 (just right = healthy), above 1.5 (too hot = fluorosis). Picture a green zone on a gauge between 0.5-1.5.

Imagine a scale: Stage 1 = white spots (mild), Stage 2 = brown stains (moderate), Stage 3 = pitting and brittle teeth (severe dental); For skeletal - joints turning from flexible to rigid like rusting hinges.
Picture three filters in sequence: 1) Sand filter (coarse), 2) Activated alumina (adsorbs fluoride like a magnet), 3) RO membrane (fine sieve catching fluoride ions).
Think of fluoride as a shy molecule that comes out more in alkaline (friendly) conditions and hides in acidic conditions. Higher pH = more fluoride released.

analogies.analogy: Like salt in food - a little is good (prevents deficiency diseases), too much makes it harmful (causes health issues). Just as we need optimal salt, we need optimal fluoride.
Like sunlight on paper - some exposure makes it stronger (prevents decay), but too much fades and damages it (causes stains and pitting).
Like a sponge that soaks up fluoride from water. When full, we squeeze it out (regenerate) with special solution and reuse it.
Like rust forming on iron - gradually makes joints stiff and bones brittle, limiting movement just as rust locks up a hinge.
